deferredEntityIds: readonly number[];\n  readonly deferredEventIds: readonly number[];\n}\n\ntype PackCandidate =\n  | { readonly type: \"entity\"; readonly priority: number; readonly entity: EntityDelta }\n  | { readonly type: \"event\"; readonly priority: number; readonly event: SnapshotEvent };\n\nfunction eventPriority(event: SnapshotEvent): number {\n  if (event.kind === EventKind.ModeEnd) return 0;\n  if (event.kind === EventKind.Kill) return 1;\n  if (\n    event.kind === EventKind.HitConfirm ||\n    event.kind === EventKind.Airshot ||\n    event.kind === EventKind.Impressive\n  ) return 2;\n  if (event.kind === EventKind.Damage) return 4;\n  return 5;\n}\n\nfunction projectilePriority(entity: EntityDelta): number {\n  if (entity.delete === true) return 0;\n  if (entity.create === true) return 3;\n  return 6;\n}\n\nfunction packWithinBudget(\n  base: Omit<SnapshotFrame, \"entities\" | \"events\">,\n  mandatory: readonly EntityDelta[],\n  optional: readonly EntityDelta[],\n  events: readonly SnapshotEvent[],\n  ceiling: number,\n): SnapshotFrame | undefined {\n  let frame: SnapshotFrame = { ...base, entities: mandatory, events: [] };\n projectilePriority(entity),\n      entity,\n    })),\n    ...events.map((event) => ({\n      type: \"event\" as const,\n      priority: eventPriority(event),\n      event,\n    })),\n  ].sort((left, right) => left.priority - right.priority ||\n    (left.type === \"entity\" ? left.entity.id : left.event.id) -\n    (right.type === \"entity\" ? right.entity.id : right.event.id));\n\n  for (const candidate of candidates) {\n    const next: SnapshotFrame = candidate.type === \"entity\"\n      ? { ...frame, entities: [...frame.entities, candidate.entity] }\n      : { ...frame, events: [...frame.events, candidate.event] };\n    if (encodeFrameForSizeProbe(next).length <= ceiling) frame = next;\n  }\n  return frame;\n}\n\nfunction representedState(\n  baseline: readonly EntityState[],\n  current: readonly EntityState[],\n  included: readonly EntityDelta[],\n  full: boolean,\n): readonly EntityState[] {\n  const currentById = new Map(current.map((entity) => [entity.id, entity]));\n  const represented = new Map(\n    (full ? [] : baseline).map((entity) => [entity.id, entity]),\n  );\n  for (const delta of included) {\n    if (delta.delete === true) represented.delete(delta.id);\n    else {\n      const entity = currentById.get(delta.id);\n      if (entity !== undefined) represented.set(delta.id, entity);\n    }\n  }\n  return [...represented.values()].sort((left, right) => left.id - right.id);\n}\n\nfunction packedResult(\n  input: PackSnapshotInput,\n  frame: SnapshotFrame,\n  promotedToFull: boolean,\n): PackedSnapshot {\n  const bytes = encodeFrame(frame);\n  const includedEntityIds = new Set(frame.entities.map((entity) => entity.id));\n  const includedEventIds = new Set(frame.events.map((event) => event.id));\n  const allDeltas = frame.full\n    ? input.entities.map((entity) => fullEntity(entity, input.selfId))\n    : deltaEntities(input.entities, input.baselineEntities, input.selfId);\n  return {\n    frame,\n    bytes,\n    promotedToFull,\n    baselineEntities: representedState(\n      input.baselineEntities,\n      input.entities,\n      frame.entities,\n      frame.full,\n    ),\n    deferredEntityIds: allDeltas\n      .filter((entity) => !includedEntityIds.has(entity.id))\n      .map((entity) => entity.id),\n    deferredEventIds: input.events\n      .filter((event) => !includedEventIds.has(event.id))\n      .map((event) => event.id),\n  };\n}\n\nexport function packSnapshot(input: PackSnapshotInput): PackedSnapshot {\n  const ceiling = input.maxBytes ?? entity.kind === EntityKind.Player)\n      .map((entity) => entity.id),\n    ...input.baselineEntities\n      .filter((entity) => entity.kind === EntityKind.Player)\n      .map((entity) => entity.id),\n  ]);\n  const deltaBase: Omit<SnapshotFrame, \"entities\" | \"events\"> = {\n    type: FrameType.Snapshot,\n    full: false,\n    tick: input.tick,\n    lastProcessedCmdSeq: input.lastProcessedCmdSeq,\n    cmdArrivalMargin: input.cmdArrivalMargin,\n    baselineEpoch: input.baselineEpoch,\n    baselineTick: input.baselineTick,\n    ...(input.modeState === undefined ? {} : { modeState: input.modeState }),\n  };\n  if (input.forceFull !== true) {\n    const mandatoryDelta = deltaEntitiesForFrame.filter(\n      (entity) => entity.self === true || playerIds.has(entity.id),\n    );\n    const optionalDelta = deltaEntitiesForFrame.filter(\n      (entity) => entity.self !== true && !playerIds.has(entity.id),\n    );\n    const delta = packWithinBudget(\n      deltaBase,\n      mandatoryDelta,\n      optionalDelta,\n      input.events,\n      ceiling,\n    );\n    if (delta !== undefined) return packedResult(input, delta, false);\n {"parentUuid":"79938b2d-061a-42f4-8ac6-b3880fa9702f","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"49eceec4-a29d-4d6b-bef9-f1f93fcaa607","agentId":"ad6a450b0f39ab436","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01URnRHJ7Kga3ZhHpMBT7Vbj","type":"tool_result","content":"(Bash completed with no output)","is_error":false}]},"uuid":"44b16341-b8ed-42b0-bc06-0bd6ce019173","timestamp":"2026-07-19T12:29:59.223Z","toolUseResult":{"stdout":"","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":true},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"79938b2d-061a-42f4-8ac6-b3880fa9702f","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Volumes/SD/gungame","sessionId":"bd0d75af-e4ac-4650-932a-6d7c12498f7e","version":"2.1.214","gitBranch":"HEAD"}
{"parentUuid":"44b16341-b8ed-42b0-bc06-0bd6ce019173","isSidechain":true,"agentId":"ad6a450b0f39ab436","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_011CdBRDX5bozjb4uFkVvYKE","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":5691,"cache_read_input_tokens":236706,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":5691,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":3,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"diagnostics":null},"requestId":"req_011CdBRDVXqSGhY6knZ1hEAD","attributionAgent":"general-purpose","type":"assistant","uuid":"a01e5f64-12da-4928-aa72-a283989bf007","timestamp":"2026-07-19T12:30:56.496Z","effort":"high","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Volumes/SD/gungame","sessionId":"bd0d75af-e4ac-4650-932a-6d7c12498f7e","version":"2.1.214","gitBranch":"HEAD"}
{"parentUuid":"a01e5f64-12da-4928-aa72-a283989bf007","isSidechain":true,"agentId":"ad6a450b0f39ab436","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_011CdBRDX5bozjb4uFkVvYKE","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"# Adversarial review — server/src + client/src @ HEAD\n\nEvery finding below is verified against the code; the top three server findings were additionally confirmed at runtime with a throwaway tsx probe (room instantiated, 256 ticks driven; probe file trashed afterwards).\n\n## P0\n\n**1. Fill bots are motionless statues — the entire godtier bot lane is dead code in production.**\n`/Volumes/SD/gungame/server/src/rooms.ts:530` gates movement/firing on `slot.peer !== undefined`, but bots are created with `peer: undefined` (`addSlot(undefined, …)` at rooms.ts:1067). `botCommand()` runs and increments `botSeq`, then the frame is discarded. **Runtime-verified:** 4 bots, 256 ticks, positions bit-identical (0,0,0), botSeq 257. Player-visible: quickplay rooms contain 4–5 named \"players\" standing frozen at spawn origin, never firing; every bot feature (aim error, reaction ticks, bot-sourced near-miss/IMPRESSIVE/airshot content) is inert. Fix: `if (frame !== undefined && (slot.peer !== undefined || slot.isBot) && slot.alive && !this.rules.frozen)`.\n\n## P1\n\n**2. Reconnect hold is actually 30 s, not the spec'd 45 s.** `kickAfk` (rooms.ts:1034–1047) doesn't skip peerless slots; a disconnected player's `lastInputMs` freezes, so the drought branch removes the slot at +30 s, before `holdUntilMs` (+45 s). **Runtime-verified: slot removed at exactly 30,000 ms.** Player-visible: reconnecting between 30–45 s silently fails; gun-game tier lost. Fix: `if (slot.isBot || slot.peer === undefined) continue;` — hold expiry owns disconnected slots.\n\n**3. Rooms are never reaped once bots exist — permanent room/CPU leak.** `desiredBotCount(0) = 5` (godtier.ts:13–15), so when the last human disconnects, `rebalanceBots` keeps/adds bots; `shouldReap` requires `players.size === 0` (rooms.ts:592–595) which is never true again. **Runtime-verified: 5 players remain, `shouldReap` false at 10 min.** Every room ever created ticks 5 bots at 64 Hz forever (and, once finding 1 is fixed, they'll also fight and generate events forever). Fix: when connected humans = 0, remove all bots (target 0), or exclude bots from the reap condition and purge them there.\n\n**4. EventJournal is unbounded for never-acking slots.** `broadcastEvent` (rooms.ts:1018–1020) adds every event to every slot including bots and held slots; bots never receive snapshots (rooms.ts:991) so `EventJournal.events` (packages/protocol/src/snapshot.ts:335–373 — no cap) grows without bound. Combined with finding 3 this is an unbounded server memory leak in every abandoned room. Fix: don't journal for peerless/bot slots (or cap the journal).\n\n**5. Quickplay/created-room reconnect never resumes — and known finding 8's fix is ABSENT.** The token is stored under `gg:reconnect:${roomId}` (client/src/net/session.ts:278) but `joinHello` looks it up via the `?room=` query param (session.ts:84–85), which quickplay/create URLs never gain: nothing in main.ts ever `replaceState`s the room into the URL (the only `history.replaceState` is for `?style=`; no `/r/:id` handling exists anywhere — grep confirms). So the auto-reconnect reload (main.ts:363–380) rejoins as a *fresh quickplay player*, possibly in a different room, while the server dutifully holds the old slot. Also the scoreboard \"copy invite\" button has no handler for non-creators (hud.ts:353–354 only wired inside `showInvite`). Fix: on Welcome, `history.replaceState` the canonical `?room=`/`/r/:id` URL — this simultaneously completes finding 8 and repairs quickplay resume.\n\n**6. Leavers remain as ghost husks, rendered forever.** `RemoteInterpolation` has no buffer-removal path (client/src/net/interpolation.ts:28–43); after a delete delta prunes the session entity map, `sample()` still returns the last buffered state for that id indefinitely, so the departed player's model + nameplate stand frozen in the world for the rest of the session (full snapshots clear `entities` but not buffers). Fix: plumb entity deletions (and full-snapshot resets) into the interpolation buffers, or drop buffers whose newest sample is older than ~1 s.\n\n**7. Pulse inputs are lost above 64 fps; sub-tick fire data mostly discarded.** `RawInput.sampleTick` (client/src/input.ts:363–380) clears `queuedJump`/`queuedFire`/`fireFraction` per **render frame**, but the sim tick (sim-bridge.ts:136–154) only consumes the *last* `applyInput` before it. At 144 Hz two frames land per tick, so ~half of wheel-down jumps vanish (bhop-critical) and most clicks lose their latched `fireFraction`/`firedYaw`/`firedPitch` — the §3.6 sub-tick contract silently degrades to frame-accumulated angles. `fireFraction` is also computed against the render-frame start, not the tick start (input.ts:389), skewing the timebase. Fix: latch pulses until a sim tick consumes them (move consumption into the sim-bridge tick).\n\n**8. Spawn selection has no occupancy check — spawning inside another player is routine.** `spawnFor` (rooms.ts:945–951) picks `(id + generation − 2) % N` deterministically with no distance/telefrag logic; with motionless bots parked at spawns (finding 1) and small pools, humans spawn inside other bodies (players don't collide with each other, so you respawn camera-inside a model, both spawn-protected). Fix: pick the candidate maximizing minimum distance to living players.\n\n**9. Per-shot/per-hit GPU resource leaks; no material is ever disposed client-side.** `showAimTracer` runs on every local hitscan shot and leaks a `LineBasicMaterial` (main.ts:531–547, geometry disposed, material not); `showTracer` leaks its line material and the impact `SphereGeometry` (main.ts:512–528). `applyStyle`/`installVisualMap` create a fresh `RenderMaterials` set per style switch/map rotation and never dispose the previous set (main.ts:188–238, 331; grep: zero `material.dispose` in client/src); `WeaponViewmodel.setWeapon` drops 5–8 geometries undisposed per tier change (viewmodels.ts:165–182). Over an hour: thousands of leaked GPU programs/buffers. Fix: shared tracer material per style; dispose impact geometry in its timeout; dispose replaced material sets and silhouette geometries.\n\n## P2\n\n**10. Bhop trial never aborts** (client/src/bhop-ghost.ts:104–139): once you brush the start gate, `recording` grows ~31 Hz unbounded and the HUD stopwatch stays visible until the full route is completed — most players never complete it. Add a timeout/death/leave-radius abort.\n\n**11. Exhausted-reconnect dead-end text** (main.ts:367–369 + hud.ts:285–288): after 3 attempts, HUD permanently shows \"reconnecting… 0\" while nothing reconnects; the rejoin button works but the label lies. Show \"connection lost — rejoin?\" instead.\n\n**12. Tick-quarantine path destroys the reconnect hold** (rooms.ts:512–515): the consume-error catch sets `slot.peer = undefined` without `holdUntilMs`, so `expireHolds` deletes the slot next tick — a transient protocol hiccup instantly costs full ladder progress. Set `holdUntilMs = nowMs + RECONNECT_HOLD_MS` there.\n\n**13. Quarantine boundary: contained at process level, but cross-room blast radius remains.** Nothing escapes to process death anymore (loop.runPhase catches step/sweep; transport message/open wrap and quarantine; the FSM clock is unified on `performance.now` — hotfix1 verified sound). But `RoomManager.tick` (rooms.ts:1204–1209) runs all rooms inside one `step()`: an exception from one room — e.g. `packSnapshot`'s mandatory-full-over-ceiling `ProtocolError` (protocol/snapshot.ts:305) reachable via the *uncaught* `sendSnapshots → openBaseline` path (rooms.ts:1006–1007; margin at 12 players × 16-char scoreboard names is thin) — starves every room after it in Map order, every tick, indefinitely. uWS `close`/`drain` callbacks are also unwrapped. Fix: per-room try/catch in `RoomManager.tick`; wrap the per-slot baseline promotion.\n\n**14. Dev-panel params desync prediction** (main.ts:429–450): the panel is seeded with SCOUTZ regardless of room config; touching any slider pushes scoutz-gravity params into prediction in a standard-gravity room → permanent rubber-banding until reload. Also default quickplay first loads Spire then swaps to Foundry (sim-bridge.ts:214–227 defaults to scoutz when `?mode` absent) — wasted double map load on the most common path.\n\n**15. Clip export container validity** (clip-capture.ts:81–95): concatenating the init chunk with clusters recorded minutes later yields webm files with absolute timestamps — wrong duration/broken seeking in most players after the first 12 s window; and `requestData()` resolves after the blob is assembled, so the final ≤500 ms (often the kill itself on hotkey export) is missing. Ring buffer memory itself is fine (~12 MB). Needs a runtime check, but the structure is wrong.\n\n**16. Near-miss dedupe never cleaned on in-tick despawns** (sim/combat.ts:551–560 use `live.delete`, bypassing `delete()`'s `nearMissSeen` cleanup at 535–538): after projectile-id wrap (0x8000–0xfffe), stale keys suppress near-miss events for reused ids; Set grows to ~400 k entries meanwhile.\n\n**17. Invite copy is once-only** (hud.ts:345–352, `{ once: true }`): second copy attempt silently no-ops.\n\n**Known-findings fix verification:** 1 (live-canvas pointer lock) ✓ present; 5 (FSM clock unification + per-connection quarantine + loop catch) ✓ present and sound; 6 (in-place style switch, replaceState-only) ✓; 7 (human-count quickplay ranking — `connectedCount` counts only peered slots, bots excluded) ✓; 9 (room-not-found card with quickplay offer) ✓; **8 (surface `/r/:id` after create) ✗ NOT fixed** — see finding 5.\n\n**Production-worthiness verdict:** Not shippable in current state. The netcode spine (FSM, epochs/resync, cmd window, backpressure, lag-comp validation, prediction/reconciliation) is genuinely solid and the hotfix1 quarantine holds at the process level — but the newest layer (5-bot fill / godtier) shipped functionally untested at runtime: bots don't move at all (P0), and their lifecycle arithmetic silently converts every room into an immortal CPU/memory leak (P1×2), while the reconnect contract the spec calls normative is broken three independent ways (30 s hold, quickplay token lookup, quarantine hold-drop). The client will feel good in a 10-minute demo and degrade over an hour (material/geometry leaks, ghost husks, eternal bhop timer) and on 144 Hz displays (dropped wheel-jumps, degraded sub-tick fire). All top findings are small, local fixes — one conditional, one `continue`, a bot-count rule, a `replaceState`, a buffer-prune, a pulse-latch — so a focused hotfix2 pass plus a one-hour soak and a 144 Hz input check would move this to shippable."}],"stop_reason":"end_turn","stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":5691,"cache_read_input_tokens":236706,"output_tokens":4283,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":5691},"inference_geo":"not_available","iterations":[{"input_tokens":2,"output_tokens":4283,"cache_read_input_tokens":236706,"cache_creation_input_tokens":5691,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":5691,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"type":"message"}],"speed":"standard"},"diagnostics":null},"requestId":"req_011CdBRDVXqSGhY6knZ1hEAD","attributionAgent":"general-purpose","type":"assistant","uuid":"a2dff659-b7b6-4932-a6ef-e406f5b018a1","timestamp":"2026-07-19T12:30:56.649Z","effort":"high","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Volumes/SD/gungame","sessionId":"bd0d75af-e4ac-4650-932a-6d7c12498f7e","version":"2.1.214","gitBranch":"HEAD"}
